text:
Exactly right. Two different thresholds:
Value
Trigger threshold
Target dwell time
Purpose
When to START rate limiting
What algorithm aims for
The Logic
Dwell time (us)
20000
- TRIGGER THRESHOLD -
15000 —
10000-
5000
- TARGET
Default
20,000 MS
Configurable (e.g., 5,000 Ms)
Normal
Rate limiting
kicks in
Stabilized
at target
Why Two Values?
Trigger (20ms): "Things are bad enough that we need to intervene"
Target (5ms): "This is healthy - don't stop limiting until we're here"
The gap provides headroom:
- If target = trigger, you'd oscillate (limit on, dwell drops, limit off, dwell spikes, repeat)
- With target « trigger, system stabilizes with
to spare before hitting danger zone again
Config Example
overload thresholds:
read_dwell_time:
per_ticket_micros: 20000
# - START limiting when this exceeded
rate_limiter:
type: PController
config:
target_dwell_time_micros: 5000 # - AIM for this while limiting
Flow Recap
1. Normal:
dwell = 2ms
→ no action
2. Getting busy: dwell = 10ms
→ no action (below 20ms trigger)
3. Overloaded:
dwell = 25ms
→ ALL 10 samples > 20ms → TRIGGER
4. Limiting:
dwell = 18ms
→ algorithm pushing toward 5ms target
5. Improving:
dwell = 8ms
→ still limiting, not at target yet
6. Stable:
dwell = 5ms
→ at target, signals clear → disable
7. Back to normal: dwell = 2ms → rate limiter OFF
You got it - hit the edge, throttle hard, don't let go until things are actually healthy again.
